title: Error: runc: runc create failed: unable to start container process: exec: "echo": executable file not found in $PATH: OCI runtime attempted to invoke a command that was not found
body:
I have set additionalimagestores. The article is
https://www.redhat.com/sysadmin/image-stores-podman.
$ podman images
REPOSITORY                                      TAG         IMAGE ID      CREATED        SIZE        R/O
docker.io/library/centos                        7.9.2009    eeb6ee3f44bd  10 months ago  212 MB      true
docker.io/library/python                        3.8         4544558e07c5  39 hours ago   936 MB      false

If I run the image which R/O is true, like the following error.
$ podman run --userns=keep-id eeb6ee3f44bd echo "hello"
Error: runc: runc create failed: unable to start container process: exec: "echo": executable file not found in $PATH: OCI runtime attempted to invoke a command that was not found

If run it without --userns=keep-id, it runs right.
$ podman run  eeb6ee3f44bd echo "hello"
hello

If run the image which R/O is false, it runs right.
$ podman run --userns=keep-id 4544558e07c5 echo "hello"
hello

comments.body:
> I have set additionalimagestores. The article is https://www.redhat.com/sysadmin/image-stores-podman. $ podman images REPOSITORY TAG IMAGE ID CREATED SIZE R/O docker.io/library/centos 7.9.2009 eeb6ee3f44bd 10 months ago 212 MB true docker.io/library/python 3.8 4544558e07c5 39 hours ago 936 MB false
> 
> If I run the image which R/O is true, like the following error. $ podman run --userns=keep-id eeb6ee3f44bd echo "hello" Error: runc: runc create failed: unable to start container process: exec: "echo": executable file not found in $PATH: OCI runtime attempted to invoke a command that was not found
> 
> If run it without --userns=keep-id, it runs right. $ podman run eeb6ee3f44bd echo "hello" hello
> 
> If run the image which R/O is false, it runs right. $ podman run --userns=keep-id 4544558e07c5 echo "hello" hello

`Error: runc: runc create failed: unable to start container process: exec: "echo": executable file not found in $PATH: OCI runtime attempted to invoke a command that was not found`
This means that there is no the echo executable in the image

One solution is to use the shell's built-in echo command:
`podman run --entrypoint sh centos -c echo "hello"`
First is the file system with the additional stores marked as NOEXEC?  Could this be an SELinux issue? Any AVCs?

$ podman images
REPOSITORY                         TAG         IMAGE ID      CREATED        SIZE        R/O
docker.io/library/centos           8.4.2105    5d0da3dc9764  10 months ago  239 MB      true

$ podman run --rm --userns=keep-id --entrypoint bash 5d0da3dc9764 echo "hello"
Error: runc: runc create failed: unable to start container process: exec: "bash": executable file not found in $PATH: OCI runtime attempted to invoke a command that was not found

I have login to the host which image R/O is false.
$ podman images
REPOSITORY                         TAG         IMAGE ID      CREATED        SIZE
docker.io/library/centos           8.4.2105    5d0da3dc9764  10 months ago  239 MB

$ podman run --rm --userns=keep-id --entrypoint bash  5d0da3dc9764 echo "hello"
Error: chown /home/rsync/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay-containers/8e68d47ef64c8908bb6bff35a2255d8451632e71144a852f617d209f2e352fe7/userdata: operation not permitted
Please show output of podman info.  Are you using an image in global storage with a rooltess container, there are potentially issues with read and search access by a rootless user.
Can I pull image from additionalimagestores to local path? The additionalimagestores is owned by rootless user, which is the same user who run the image.
have you chowned the additional store to 0755 before using it from rootless? `chmod -R 0755 /path/to/the/additional/store`?

> No, I haven't do that.
I have set force_mask="700" through by storage.conf for additionalimagestores.
Yes, I change it to 0755, and it works. Thanks.
